title: Acids and bases
summary: 9701 A Level acid–base theory — Ka, pH, buffers, and titration curves with PhET acid–base sim.
learningObjectives: Apply Brønsted–Lowry theory to conjugate pairs.
Calculate pH of strong and weak acids/bases.
Explain buffer action and construct buffer equations.

simpleExplanation.title: A Level acid–base
simpleExplanation.summary: Weak acids partially dissociate — Ka quantifies strength. Buffers resist pH change via conjugate acid–base pairs.
steps: Strong–strong titration: sharp rise at equivalence pH 7.

sections.content: **A Level acids and bases** (25.1) extends AS **7.2**. **Buffer** and **titration curve** questions are high mark — sketch curves with **equivalence point** labelled.

sections.label: Mark an acids and bases question
